🌅 Morning Briefing

Saturday, March 21, 2026

🌍 Global News
Iran fires missiles at UK-US Diego Garcia military base
Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at the U.S.-U.K. military base on Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean. The attack was unsuccessful and both missiles failed to hit the target. The strike came after the UK authorized the U.S. to use British bases to carry out strikes on Iranian missile sites targeting ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
Reuters / BBC / The Guardian · war
UK approves US use of British bases to strike Iran missile sites
The British government authorized the U.S. to use military bases in Britain to carry out strikes on Iranian missile sites that are attacking ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The authorization includes RAF Fairford in Gloucestershire and Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean for defensive operations.
Reuters · war
Russian drone attack kills two in Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine
A Russian drone attack on a residential area in Zaporizhzhia killed a man and a woman, with two children aged 11 and 15 also injured. The attack occurred ahead of planned Ukraine-U.S. talks and highlights ongoing civilian casualties in the conflict.
BBC / The Hindu · war
South Korea factory fire kills 14, injures nearly 60
A massive fire at a car parts factory in Daejeon, South Korea killed at least 14 people and injured nearly 60 others. The blaze, likely caused by an explosion, engulfed a three-floor plant. Some victims jumped from the burning building to escape, while others suffered toxic smoke inhalation.
AP / The Guardian / New York Times · disaster
BTS holds massive comeback concert in Seoul
K-pop group BTS performed together for the first time since October 2022 at a free comeback concert in Gwanghwamun Square, Seoul. The 'Arirang' concert set a record for the largest-ever public concert in South Korea, drawing hundreds of thousands of fans. The event was also streamed live on Netflix.
CNN / New York Times · other
